title: The Briefing
tagline: AI intelligence for Gauntlet AI — April 14, 2026

items.headline: The Model Anthropic Built and Can't Ship Yet
items.summary: Anthropic launched Project Glasswing — a 40+ partner initiative built around Mythos Preview, a frontier model so capable at coding and reasoning it became the best vulnerability-hunter alive. It's already found thousands of critical flaws across every major OS and browser, including a 27-year-old bug in OpenBSD. The UK's AI Security Institute confirmed Mythos passed every stage of their most rigorous evaluation — the first model to do so — which is also why Anthropic won't release it publicly yet.

items.headline: Claude Is Being Quietly Throttled by GPU Load
items.summary: An AMD Senior AI Director tracked Claude's performance from January through March and found median thinking depth dropped from ~2,200 to ~600 characters. API requests went up 80x — not from higher usage, but from more failures and retries. Worst hours: 5pm and 7pm PST. Workaround: switch to Sonnet, avoid Opus at peak hours, don't run idle sessions over an hour.

items.headline: Anthropic Building a Lovable Competitor
items.summary: Leaked screenshots showed a full-stack app builder coming to Claude — drag-and-drop, build and deploy, the whole thing. The original post pulled 5.2 million views with "sneak peek at something coming soon to Claude :)". If you've built a product in the make-it-easy-to-build-apps-with-Claude category, this week changed your competitive picture.

items.headline: Google's Desktop Agent, Before I/O
items.summary: A new Agent tab has appeared in Gemini Enterprise: set a goal, connect your apps, toggle human review. The design closely mirrors Claude Cowork. Still in testing, but Google I/O is just weeks away — expect a formal launch announcement then.

items.headline: The Business That Runs While You Sleep
items.summary: Someone used an open-source AI agent to build a fully automated sales machine for pool installations. It finds homes worth $500k–$1.2M without pools, renders a pool in the backyard using AI, and mails a physical before-and-after postcard to the homeowner — no salesperson, no ad budget. Greg Isenberg shared the example alongside 10 more like it, all running the same playbook: find the data gap, build before they ask, show up with the math done.

items.headline: HeyGen: Turn Any Agent Output Into a Video
items.summary: HeyGen launched a tool that takes whatever your AI agent produces and turns it into a video with an avatar presenter. One CEO's setup: an AI agent does overnight research, HeyGen turns it into a morning briefing delivered by a custom AI avatar. The agent-to-human handoff is currently a wall of text — this replaces it.

items.headline: Claude Cowork 101: Automate Your Workday Without Writing Code
items.summary: A practical walkthrough of setting up Claude Cowork from scratch and starting to automate real workflows — no technical background needed. Also: a companion video on sharing Cowork skills across a team with three methods suited to different org sizes.

items.headline: Tenex Is Hiring: AI Strategist, Forward Deployed Engineer & More
items.summary: Tenex (the team behind the ultrathink newsletter) is hiring across four roles: AI Strategist, Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI Engineer, and Engagement Manager. Salary varies by role and experience with additional comp based on output. Must be NY-based.

items.headline: Google I/O Is Weeks Away — Expect the Gemini Agent Tab to Go Public
items.summary: The Agent tab spotted in Gemini Enterprise is still in testing, but Google I/O's timing makes an announcement highly likely. Teams already building Cowork-style workflows should watch this space closely.
